vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-479: OpenRouter integration — API key settings + model selection in Mission Control

plan.title: OpenRouter integration — API key settings + model selection in Mission Control
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Mission Control only supports 3 hardcoded Claude models. Users want access to the full spectrum of LLMs available through OpenRouter (Qwen, DeepSeek, Llama, Mistral, etc.) including free models, while keeping the existing Claude models as first-class options.
narratives.Proposal: Add OpenRouter as a full provider in the existing multi-provider system. Create a backend service for model discovery with caching, a dedicated Settings page for API key and model favorites, unify the ModelPicker to show all enabled providers, and modify conversation/agent launchers to set Anthropic SDK env overrides when spawning OpenRouter model sessions.

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/services/openrouter-service.ts. Implement: (1) fetchModels() — GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models with in-memory cache (5min TTL), (2) validateApiKey() — test key with a lightweight API call, (3) getModelCapabilities() — extract thinking/tool support from model metadata. Use async fetch (no blocking calls). Follow existing service patterns from tracker-config.ts.

subItems.title: fetchModels returns typed model list with id, name, pricing, context_length, capabilities
Model list is cached in memory with 5-minute TTL, subsequent calls return cache
validateApiKey returns success/error without throwing
Model capabilities include supports_thinking boolean derived from model metadata

narrative.Action: Add 'openrouter' to ModelProvider enum in model-capabilities.ts. Add openrouter API key field to config-yaml.ts schema and NormalizedConfig. Add OPENROUTER_API_KEY extraction to env-loader.ts getApiKeysFromEnv(). Add openrouter to settings-api.ts conversion functions. Add openrouter favorites array to config schema (openrouter.favorites: string[]).

subItems.title: ModelProvider enum includes 'openrouter' and it's recognized throughout the settings pipeline
OPENROUTER_API_KEY is loaded from ~/.panopticon.env and config.yaml with correct priority
OpenRouter favorites list persists in config.yaml across restarts

items.title: Add OpenRouter API endpoints to settings routes

narrative.Action: Add to src/dashboard/server/routes/settings.ts: (1) GET /api/settings/openrouter/models — fetch available models via openrouter-service, (2) PUT /api/settings/openrouter/favorites — save/update favorite model IDs, (3) POST /api/settings/openrouter/test-key — validate API key. Follow existing Effect.js httpHandler pattern. Ensure GET /api/settings includes openrouter state.

subItems.title: GET /api/settings/openrouter/models returns model list with pricing and capabilities
PUT /api/settings/openrouter/favorites persists favorites to config.yaml
POST /api/settings/openrouter/test-key returns validation result

items.title: Create dedicated OpenRouter settings page with model browser

narrative.Action: Create new React components: (1) OpenRouterPage.tsx — dedicated settings page with API key input + test button at top, model catalog below, (2) OpenRouterModelBrowser.tsx — searchable/filterable model list with star-to-favorite toggle, category filters (Free/Chat/Code), pricing display per model. Favorites section pinned at top. Add navigation to this page from SettingsPage. Use existing Settings component patterns.

subItems.title: API key field validates on save and shows success/error state
Model browser shows all OpenRouter models with search and category filters
Starring a model adds it to favorites, which persist across page reloads
Each model shows pricing info (cost per million tokens) and context length

items.title: Unify ModelPicker with full provider system

narrative.Action: Refactor ModelPicker.tsx to: (1) fetch available models from GET /api/settings/available-models (all enabled providers) plus OpenRouter favorites, (2) group by provider with section headers (Anthropic, OpenRouter, OpenAI, etc.), (3) show per-model cost indicator, (4) update EffortPicker.tsx to check model capability metadata for effort support instead of hardcoded MODEL_EFFORT_SUPPORT map. Update DraftConversationPanel.tsx to pass provider info alongside model ID.

subItems.title: ModelPicker shows models from all enabled providers grouped by provider name
OpenRouter favorites appear in the OpenRouter section of the picker
EffortPicker dynamically enables/disables based on selected model's capabilities
Per-model cost indicator visible in the dropdown

items.title: OpenRouter env overrides in conversation and agent launchers

narrative.Action: Modify spawnConversationSession() in conversations.ts: detect if selected model is an OpenRouter model (check against favorites list or model ID pattern). If so, add ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=<key>, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='' to the launcher script. Apply same pattern to agent launcher scripts in agents.ts for work agent integration. Native provider models continue using existing flow unchanged.

subItems.title: Selecting an OpenRouter model spawns a session with correct ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and AUTH_TOKEN env vars
Native Claude models continue working without any env var overrides
Work agent launchers support OpenRouter models via model override settings
End-to-end test: spawn conversation with qwen/qwen3.6-plus:free, receive a response

items.title: Cost tracking and usage display for OpenRouter models

narrative.Action: Implement cost visibility: (1) show per-model pricing in ModelPicker from OpenRouter model metadata (prompt/completion cost per million tokens), (2) display pricing in the OpenRouter Settings model browser. Track OpenRouter API usage if the OpenRouter API provides usage/billing endpoints, otherwise display static pricing only for v1.

subItems.title: ModelPicker shows cost-per-million-tokens for OpenRouter models
Free models are clearly labeled as FREE in both picker and settings browser

items.title: Quality gates: typecheck, lint, and test pass

narrative.Action: Ensure npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass with the new code. Fix any type errors from the provider system changes. Add unit tests for openrouter-service (model fetching, caching, key validation).

subItems.title: npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run lint passes
npm test passes including new OpenRouter service tests
